ll:f4a815d8cbb4.message_template: SAMEPORT mode enabled (gRPC shares listener with REST via cmux/protocol).\n

ll:984906235ac9.message_template: Config: REST={}  gRPC=http://{}  model={}  (post-build quality parity + edge cases)\n\n

ll:c966aa4c5395.message_template: gRPC cancel test (mid-stream): cleanly_stopped={} chunks_before_cancel={} partial_prompt_tokens={} partial_completion_tokens={}\n

ll:b188aec787fd.message_template: \n\n=== COMPARATIVE METRICS TABLE (side-by-side REST vs gRPC; expect token parity) ===

ll:c100f1872f4c.message_template: \n\n=== DETAILED TECHNICAL COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS REPORT (REAL gRPC DATA + EDGES) ===

ll:f406f982eb4b.message_template: - Side-by-side token/metrics parity for transport validation (post-fix).

ll:51c779d806bc.message_template: - Mid-gen stream cancellation + token counts (simple_user_prompt cancel subcase).

ll:9ae403afcb9d.message_template: - OTEL/metrics/pprof exposure check (if server surfaces them; otelconnect spans in logs/collector).

ll:55a98a3637fa.message_template: - SAMEPORT + separate port support via flags/env.

ll:a2058632b604.message_template: - Runnable post `make local` / build-local.sh + `./ollama serve` (with OLLAMA_GRPC_*).

ll:8ed64d607a3f.message_template: {} stream={}: in={} out={} tot={}ms tps={} ttft={}ms wreq={} wresp={} done={} tools={} struct={} cancel={} chunks={}\n

ll:6d6b3dff71d5.message_template: {} non-stream: prompt_tokens_identical={} (REST={} gRPC={}), wire_delta_req={} bytes (gRPC typically compact for tools)\n

ll:6db9c1aea34d.message_template: \nContext overhead note: Server-side prompt_eval_count / eval_count are identical (or near) because message normalization + inference happens after the protocol adapter in the shared core (*Server + scheduler + llama). gRPC (protobuf + Connect) compactness reduces client-side serialization cost and wire bytes vs JSON, giving app-layer headroom for richer agent state (tools, history, vision).

ll:3d5c94197282.message_template: \nEfficiency: Compute side (tokens, TPS, runner llama-server) is shared/identical. Differences are only in wire framing (proto vs json), client overhead, and transport (h2c flow control + backpressure in gRPC streams).

ll:7c14ec41d96b.message_template: \nUse cases: REST for compatibility/debug/curl/OpenAI SDKs; gRPC for typed, cancellable, efficient agentic flows (tools in-stream), better observability (OTEL spans via otelconnect, rich status), backpressure, and microservice clients.

ll:97d136256e17.message_template: \nAdditional metrics captured: TTFT (time to first token, critical for UX), cancellation responsiveness (prevents wasted GPU cycles on mid-gen client disconnects; see cancel subtest with partial token counts), tool fidelity (shared converters ensure same tool_calls count), structured validity (json format roundtrips), multi-turn growth, client retry paths, server health (gRPC listener, OTEL, runner discovery via shared sched).

ll:1b20436eb6ce.message_template: \nCancellation edge summary: {} runs; clean stops reported in gRPC mid-stream (tokens partial but stop respected; expect no further generation post-cancel).\n

ll:77390c0b0f66.message_template: \n=== Observability / OTEL / Metrics / Edge Check (if exposed) ===

ll:81b67c1dc127.message_template: -> goroutine count sample: {} (use during concurrent gRPC+REST load to check for leaks vs baseline)\n

ll:675384466064.message_template: OTEL note: Spans/metrics for gRPC (and shared paths) come from otelconnect interceptors + OTel SDK (if OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER/OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER or equiv configured in env).

ll:705f735ceb82.message_template: Look in server logs for 'component=grpc' + reason + stream_id + tokens + duration_ms (rich slog from reliable overlay).

ll:293ced8aab8b.message_template: For full spans: run with OTEL exporter (e.g. to collector or console) and/or inspect pprof while running quality cases.

ll:32462991bea6.message_template: gRPC-specific: health/reflection (if enabled) also aid discovery; use grpcurl or buf curl for live surface.
